scope_note: Dartmouth College does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff or admitted-student GPA distribution on the cited official pages used here. The policy section stays official; the estimate card below is third-party and directional.
policy_summary: Dartmouth does not publish a minimum GPA on the cited official admissions pages. Dartmouth says a top secondary school GPA can predict college success, but it evaluates grade trends and transcript context rather than using GPA alone as a cutoff.

what_mit_does_say.claim: Dartmouth does not publish a minimum GPA on the cited official admissions pages. Dartmouth says a top secondary school GPA can predict college success, but it evaluates grade trends and transcript context rather than using GPA alone as a cutoff.

recommended_gpa.summary: Dartmouth does not publish HS GPA data for its incoming class on the cited official pages. The estimate below is a third-party directional reference, not an official Dartmouth cutoff.

estimates.label: Weighted (estimate)
estimates.method: Estimated by PrepScholar because the university did not officially report an average incoming high-school GPA on the cited admissions pages.

recommended_gpa.interpretation: This estimate suggests that successful applicants usually present extremely strong grades, but Dartmouth's official guidance still points readers back to transcript context and grade trends instead of a hard minimum.
